div.h2: Single Parameter Update: p.data ← p.data - η × p.grad

div.p: ⚠️ CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL The update MUST mutate p.data directly: python
div.h1: WRONG: Creates new Value, doesn't update original!
div.p: Direct mutation avoids adding the update to the computational graph, preventing memory leaks during long training runs.
Parameter has current value and computed gradient from backward pass. Gradient points toward higher loss (uphill direction).
Loss Landscape At p=2.0, the loss curve slopes upward. We want to move in the opposite direction.
p.data = p.data - η × p.grad p.data = 2.000 - 0.1 × 0.500 p.data = 2.000 - 0.050 p.data = 1.950
Learning Rate η = 0.1 Controls step size. Too large: overshoot minimum Too small: slow convergence
Change Applied Δp = -0.050 Negative change because:
• Gradient points uphill (toward higher loss) • SGD subtracts gradient (moves downhill) • Learning rate scales the step size • Parameter mutated in-place (no new Value)
